summary: Needs changes: the run initially claimed homogeneous-dual review while planning one generalist plus one focused reviewer; trigger wording also drifted.

findings.title: Review topology evidence contradicts the planned reviewer set
findings.why_it_matters: Homogeneous-dual requires two generalist reviewers with the same substantive prompt, packet, rubric, role contract and output schema; the planned set was one generalist plus one focused reviewer.
findings.recommendation: Either run two true homogeneous generalists or reclassify the run to an existing focused topology and keep artifacts consistent.
findings.blocker_path: Run review topology evidence -> incorrect homogeneous-dual claim -> review gate evidence becomes internally contradictory -> acceptance would rely on a misclassified review gate.
findings.acceptance_impact: The run could not honestly claim its selected review topology.

findings.title: Boundary Trace trigger lists drift across artifacts
findings.why_it_matters: Lightweight guidance works only if the contract, protocol and template describe the same trigger set.
findings.recommendation: Align the trigger bullets without adding a new schema, parser or validator.
findings.no_blocker_path_reason: The issue overlaps G-001 and is actionable as consistency cleanup after G-001 is accepted.

self_declared_limitations: Reviewer did not run tests or validators.
